what_it_does: An HTML/CSS desktop mockup of the inspector panel for a process node, with the model enum field opened to show a live, registry-fed chat-model fleet and a separately handled embed-model endpoint.
observations: Header comment frames the file as a staging probe for journey J2 step E1, not a canonical design.
Right-hand inspector panel renders an opened model enum field bound to a selected process ask node.
Chat fleet lists 15 models grouped by cloud/local suffixes, with deepseek-v4-pro:cloud marked as default brain.
Explicit note that Gemini models are hard-filtered at the transport layer.
Embed fleet is shown as a distinct endpoint with only the audit-named default BAAI/bge-m3 and a fail-loud banner if DEFAULT_EMBED_URL is unconfigured.
Uses CSS custom properties from ../design-system.css and data-ui-ref attributes for UI addressing.
Footer reports 15 models from the chat endpoint and Gemini filtered.
standouts: Strong emphasis on endpoint honesty: chat and embed are separate, with no silent fallback.
Fail-loud embed error state is surfaced as a first-class UI element rather than an empty or guessed list.
Heavy inline grounding comments tie every list and behavior to specific code references and inventory lines.
conventions: Follows project naming pattern P4/E1/J2 and references feature IDs, addresses, and source lines in comments.
Uses semantic CSS variables and a flat, BEM-like class naming convention.
Breaks the convention of keeping mockups purely visual by embedding extensive behavioral and audit annotations.
concerns: The mockup mixes presentation markup with dense legal/grounding comments, making the file hard to scan.
Some data-ui-ref values, such as ui://settings/Canvas with a capital C, appear inconsistent with the lowercase convention used elsewhere.
The local grouping label is applied to models whose names end in :latest or contain slashes, which may not all be locally hosted.
notes: A heavily annotated desktop mockup that uses the model-field picker to demonstrate registry-fed, endpoint-distinct fleet selection with a fail-loud embed state.
questions: Is the chat fleet list manually copied from MAP.md or generated from the registry at runtime?
What action opens the embed fail-loud banner versus the normal embed list?
Are the cloud/local suffix groupings derived from model names or from separate metadata?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to depict the operator picking from live chat and embed fleets; the file does that, but it also functions as an audit and grounding document, which is more than a typical mockup.
apparent_themes: Registry-driven UI rather than hard-coded model lists.
Honest representation of unknown or unenumerated state.
Traceability from design artifact to code references.
intention_signals: The file is trying to prove that the model picker can be fed directly from a live registry while keeping chat and embed endpoints strictly separate, and to make embed endpoint misconfiguration visible instead of silently degrading.
novelty: It treats a UI mockup as a grounded behavioral specification, surfacing a real inventory discrepancy (bge-m3 not in the MAP.md list) and a fail-loud error state as part of the design artifact.
contribution: A concrete desktop inspector layout and interaction pattern for a registry-backed model slot, plus a visual pattern for exposing endpoint misconfiguration as an explicit error rather than a guessed fallback.
summary_for_embedding: company/design/mockups/E1-fleet-desktop.html is an annotated HTML/CSS desktop mockup of the inspector's live model-field picker, showing a chat fleet list from the registry, a separate embed endpoint with a fail-loud unconfigured banner, and Gemini-filtered notes.
intention_for_embedding: To specify the J2/E1 journey moment where an operator selects a model from distinct live chat and embed fleets bound to a process node.
